⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 Kan : An Israeli source confirmed that advanced contacts are under way for a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, which could take effect in the coming days. Israel is not ruling out a U.S. request for a temporary truce in exchange for a commitment to resume fighting if no deal is reached with Iran, and as long as Hezbollah fire stops. The security cabinet is meeting now, with many ministers opposed. The U.S. request was raised during ambassador‑level talks in Washington. @wfwitness

The whole unit was placed on the ground" Hezbullah gives details of the event: IED and gunfire في إطار التصدّي لمحاولة توغّل جيش العدوّ الإسرائيليّ في مدينة بنت جبيل، وأثناء محاولة قوّة من الكتيبة 101، لواء المظليين، التقدّم من بلدة مارون الراس باتجاه الأحياء الجنوبيّة الغربيّة لمدينة بنت جبيل فجر الثلاثاء 14-04-2026 ، وعند وصول القوّة إلى نقطة المقتل في كمين معدّ مسبقًا، فجّر المجاهدون عبوّة ناسفة فيها، ثم خاضوا اشتباكًا معها من مسافة صفر بالأسلحة الخفيفة والمتوسّطة ما أسفر عن وقوع أفرادها بين قتيل وجريح. عمد جيش العدو إلى سحب الإصابات من منطقة المكمن تحت غطاء ناريّ ودخانيّ كثيف باتّجاه بلدة مارون الراس ومنها إلى الداخل الفلسطينيّ المحتلّ باستعمال المروحيّات، في المقابل استهدفت مدفعيّة المقاومة منطقة الإخلاء بالصواريخ والقذائف المدفعيّة. إنّ هذا الردّ سيستمرّ إلى أن يتوقّف العدوان الإسرائيليّ الأمريكيّ على بلدنا وشعبنا. ﴿وَمَا النَّصْرُ إِلاَّ مِنْ عِندِ اللّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيم﴾‏ الأربعاء 15-04-2026 ‏ 26 شوال 1447 هـ

🇺🇦 Budanov continues to make statements that contradict Zelensky's official line Today the head of the Presidential Office stated that the return of Ukrainians from abroad is only possible if there are real security guarantees and economic conditions. "No one will return just because. People are waiting for real security guarantees and economic grounds," he noted. According to him, the country needs to develop a high-value-added economy to create conditions for the return of citizens. Zelensky recently stated in Germany that conscripted Ukrainians should be returned from abroad to be sent to the front. Budanov also made it clear that he is against the mobilization of women, which has been increasingly discussed lately. "While some men are fighting and working, and others are hiding or have gone abroad - it is women who have taken on responsibility and become the foundation that sustains our rear and economy. And this is confirmed by the figures," he said. Earlier Budanov spoke against pressure on the UOC. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to OSINT Open Source Intel RT @MsMelChen : The first time I was flying to Beirut, the desk officer at London Heathrow asked before checking us in, “have you been to Israel?” We had rehearsed the answer to this question before. But Winston can't lie, so he said yes. I gave him the dirty look. There goes our vacation! "Well, you don't have the stamp on your passports so just make sure you tell the officer in Beirut that you haven't," she intoned. I was stressed out for the next 5 hours, and even more so when we had to face the border officer who, by the grace of God, did not ask us THE question (even though he took our passports to a secondary office for extra checks). Spending time in Beirut, you realize that it's the same Mediterranean light that bathes Tel Aviv; the sea is the same shade of shimmering blue because... well, it's the same sea. In both places, young people spill out of clubs at sunrise, the bass still thumping from rooftops that overlook the same ancient coastline. Both cities pulse with the same Levantine hunger for life: the clink of arak glasses, endless plates of hummus swirled with olive oil, the sudden eruption of dabke or house music that pulls strangers into a circle. Parties start on the rooftops of Gemmayze in Beirut and tumble down into Mar Mikhael’s narrow alleys; in Tel Aviv they begin on the sand at Gordon Beach and migrate to the warehouses of the Florentin district. These are both stylish people who love life, and who love to party. The energy is truly infectious. The accents may differ but something about this weird combination along with a deep sense of rootedness in community and the extended family really underscore how similar they were. And yet, there's been a wall between these two peoples. There are no flights stitching the 45 min hop across the water. No commercial trucks rumbling between the ports. Lebanese law forbids its citizens - inside the country or in the diaspora - from so much as speaking to an Israeli, a rule so absolute that some Lebanese friends of mine who live in Europe still glance over their shoulders before typing a reply to any Israeli even outside the country, whether for business or pleasure. I spent evenings in Beirut listening to Lebanese friends speak of Israelis not as the enemy but as people caught in the same endless loop of fear and longing. Decades of Hezbollah’s shadow have hollowed out parts of Lebanon, turning the south into a garrison and the economy into a ruin. Yet in the cafés of Achrafieh and the mountain villages above the city you hear it more and more: a quiet, exhausted recognition that the real hostage-takers are not across the border but inside it. I keep imagining the day the question at Beirut airport changes. I keep picturing the first flight from Rafic Harari to Ben Gurion. One day the music will be louder than the fear. One day the Lebanese and the Israelis will throw the party the rest of the world has been waiting for. I hope this is the first step: History in the making: Israel’s ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter and Lebanon’s ambassador meet for the first round of Israel Lebanon talks. This is very interesting. https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2044069712171065705/photo/1 - Open Source Intel tweet

Open Source Intel Important note on X algorithm changes (this may affect many of you): X is now applying heavy reach reductions, up to 90% impression deductions, on reposted or third party content, including TV clips and simple excerpts which are very important in our reporting (ex. video Statements from heads of state) The platform is clearly pushing for more original, native videos, especially talking videos with your own voice on camera. One example X highlighted was a man from Germany explaining why he left his home, or voiceover style reaction videos, closer to TikTok videos. (We happen to be in pre production for long form original video content, but for videos we may prefer YouTube, unless X really can compete, as production costs are very high) My feed has always been real time, verified security reporting. Fast alerts with primary sources, added context, and careful verification. We do not just copy and paste. Every post is checked. We have published over 95,000 posts. We are not perfect, and if we get something wrong, we will publicly correct it, like we always have. That said, X stated that news accounts providing rapid updates would not be limited in their reach, but we have already seen a sharp drop in reach, and it may decline further as these changes roll out. We have expanded our team and will continue to invest in accuracy, stronger verification, and proprietary intel. The mission stays the same. Delivering fast, reliable updates on the Middle East, geopolitics, and critical developments. If you want to keep seeing these updates in your feed: • You can turn on notifications • Like, reply, or engage If this work matters to you, you can quote this post and let X know that reducing reach on verified, real time news accounts hurts access to information when it matters most. The mission does not change. Thank you for being here. Jay tweet

WSJ: After the killing of Ali Khamenei, power in Iran has moved not toward moderation but toward more extreme hard-liners. His son, Mojtaba Khamenei, now leads a system increasingly dominated by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. Instead of opening space for compromise, the war strengthened radical factions. These leaders are more repressive at home—cracking down on dissent—and more aggressive abroad, showing little interest in diplomacy. Key figures in the new leadership, including Ahmad Vahidi and Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, have histories of violence and strong anti-West views.

⚡️ 🇷🇺 Defence Blog: Satellite imagery has confirmed significant structural damage at a key facility involved in producing Russia’s Sukhoi Su-57 following a recent fire. The images show multiple sections of the roof collapsed at a workshop inside the Komsomolsk-on-Amur Aviation Plant (KnAAZ), indicating the fire caused extensive internal damage rather than a limited incident. The affected facility is responsible for manufacturing polymer composite components critical to the Su-57 airframe, including structural parts essential for stealth characteristics. Depending on the extent of the damage it could further strain Russia's fifth generation fighter program. @wfwitness

WarTranslated Russia's Gazprom has delivered its second shipment of liquefied natural gas from the Baltic "Portovaya" plant to China since sanctions were lifted, according to LSEG data. The LNG carrier Valera took on the cargo at the plant on January 25 and delivered it to the Beihai LNG terminal on Wednesday. Another sanctioned Russian facility, "Arctic LNG 2," is also supplying gas to the Beihai terminal. https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/russia-delivers-second-post-sanctions-lng-cargo-portovaya-china-data-shows-2026-04-15/ tweet
